title: Linear Programming and Constrained Optimization · Mixed Practice Return
summary: Choose a fair production and distribution plan under limited resources. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for an explainable constrained optimization model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define decision variables, objective, constraints, units, and feasible domain.
Solve two-variable linear programs graphically and verify corner candidates.
Encode a constrained model for a solver and independently validate its solution.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

stimulus.title: Optimization Ethics case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Use a changed review case derived from this module: math intermediate optimization. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A mathematically optimal plan is automatically fair.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits. Artifact under review: an explainable constrained optimization model.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Optimization Ethics, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Record what required effort
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Optimization Ethics applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Evaluate whose costs and benefits an objective includes or omits.
